title: 3 of the Most Attractive Dividend Stocks in the Utilities Space
text: At a cursory glance, the concept of dividend stocks – particularly in the boring utilities space – seems overly cautious. After all, several risk-on asset classes, including technology-focused securities and cryptocurrencies have soared over the past several weeks.

title: Xcel Energy Inc. Board Declares Dividend on Common Stock
text: MINNEAPOLIS--(BUSINESS WIRE)--The Board of Directors of Xcel Energy Inc. (NASDAQ: XEL) today declared a quarterly dividend on its common stock of 52 cents per share. The dividends are payable January 20, 2024, to shareholders of record on December 28, 2023. Xcel Energy is a major U.S. electricity and natural gas company, with operations in 8 Western and Midwestern states. Xcel Energy provides a comprehensive portfolio of energy-related products and services to 3.8 million electricity customers.

title: Xcel Energy (XEL) & Ford to Install 30,000 EV Charging Ports
text: Xcel Energy (XEL) collaborates with Ford Pro to support the installation of 30,000 EV charging ports in its service territories across the United States by 2030.

title: Ford Pro partners with Xcel Energy to install 30,000 EV charging ports in the U.S.
text: Ford Motor Company (NYSE:F)'s commercial division Ford Pro has partnered with electricity provider Xcel Energy on the rollout of 30,000 electric vehicle (EV) charging ports across the United States by 2030. The two companies said Tuesday that their new 30x30 initiative under Xcel Energy's broader Electric Vehicle Supply Infrastructure (EVSI) will scale EV adoption and improve access to charging infrastructure for business fleets across Xcel Energy's U.S.

title: Ford commercial unit, Xcel Energy partner up to install 30,000 EV charging ports
text: Ford's commercial unit will collaborate with utility firm Xcel Energy to install 30,000 electric vehicle (EV) charging ports across the U.S. by 2030, the companies said on Tuesday.

title: Xcel Energy: A Blue-Chip Utility Worth Buying
text: Businesses with steady demand for their products or services benefit from more reliable earnings growth. Xcel Energy's ongoing EPS edged 1.6% higher in the nine months ended September 30. The company is financially solid, which is why it possesses an A- credit rating from S&P on a stable outlook.
